name: Xun You (Gongda)
page_title: Xun You (Gongda) 荀攸 (公達) [Han, Dong Zhuo, Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: He provided strategies instrumental in defeating Yuan Shao to Cao Cao.

sections.premium-external-linkage:
A Historical Biography of Xun You (ZZTJ Compilation)
Xun You’s SGZ
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Xun You was styled Gongda.

He was born in 157 as the son of Xun Yi (荀彝), who was a provincial clerk, and the grandson of Xun Tan, who was the Grand Administrator of Guangling. His father died when he was young, so he was cared for by his uncle, Xun Qu. Xun Qu got violent when he was drunk. He once injured Xun You in the ear by accident when Xun You was six or seven years old. For a while, Xun You would hide the ear from Xun Qu whenever he went in and out to play. Xun Qu was shocked by his behavior, thinking he was intelligent for his age.

In 169, Xun Tan died. A tomb was built for him and one of his former officials, Zhang Quan (張權), requested to guard it. Xun You, only twelve years old, told Xun Qu, “That official has an abnormal expression. He probably did something evil!” Zhang Quan was interrogated by Xun Qu and confessed to being on the run after killing someone. Xun Qu regarded Xun You as extraordinary from this incident.

In 189, He Jin summoned Xun You, among many other famous people, to Luoyang. Xun You became Attending Gentleman of the Yellow Gates. He Jin was killed soon after and a general named Dong Zhuo seized power. He then moved the capital from Luoyang to Chang’an in 190 after facing serious internal problems. Xun You followed along the journey. Xun You soon plotted with He Yong, Zheng Tai, Zhong Ji, Wu Qiong. They said, “Dong Zhuo is immoral, more so than the past Kings Jie and Zhou. The entire world hates him. Although he has amassed a strong army, he is really just one fool. Now we should just assassinate him so as to apologize to the common masses. Thereafter, we would occupy Mount Xiao and Hangu pass. We would then assist with imperial commands to order the world. This is what Duke Huan of Qi and Duke Wen of Jin did.” This plot was leaked and Xun You ended up imprisoned. He was unfazed, eating and drinking as usual. He would either be released in 192 when Dong Zhuo was killed or let out after he sent people to convince Dong Zhuo.

He left the government for a brief time to go back to his hometown before being summoned back to serve. Xun You was promoted to become Chancellor of Rencheng due to being recognized for his high ability, but he did not go. Instead, Xun You wanted to become Grand Administrator of Shu Commandery because it was a remote and safe location with a thriving population. However, he was unable to go there due to the blocked roads. He stayed in Jing province while awaiting a chance to go to Shu commandery. Xun Yu recognized his talent and recommended him to Cao Cao. Cao Cao wrote a letter to Xun You, which stated, “The world is currently in great chaos. This is the time for intelligent scholars to toil their hearts. Instead, you are turning towards the Shu-Hanzhong region, watching for opportune vagaries within it. This will not last you!” Xun You answered his summons and served as Grand Administrator of Runan. He later became Secretariat.

Xun You and Cao Cao once talked in depth. Later, Cao Cao told Zhong Yao, “Xun Gongda is no ordinary man. As I can now formulate matters with him, how could the world be cause for concern?”

Xun You later became Military Advisor (軍師). In 198, he followed Cao Cao in a campaign against Zhang Xiu. Xun You told Cao Cao, “Zhang Xiu and Liu Biao support each other in order to be strong, but Zhang Xiu and his roving army are reliant on food from Liu Biao. Liu Biao will be unable to supply him, so there will inevitably be a rift. Better to slow down our army and wait for them, then we can entice and bring them to us. But if we make a quick attack, they will certainly aid each other.” Cao Cao refused this advice and advanced his army to Rang, fighting there. However, he could not make any progress after Liu Biao reinforced the desperate Zhang Xiu. Cao Cao told Xun You, “I did not employ your words, so it came to this.” Cao Cao later defeated them using an ambush, presumably suggested by Xun You.

Yuan Shao and Cao Cao would fall out and they became bitter enemies. In the second lunar month of 200, Yuan Shao sent Yan Liang in an invasion of Cao Cao. Yan Liang then besieged Baima. Cao Cao would take action against them two months later. Xun You suggested regarding Yuan Shao, “Currently, our forces are weak, unable to compete. Dividing his power is appropriate. When your Excellency arrives at Yan ferry, if you lead the ferried troops to his rear, Yuan Shao will certainly go west in response. After that, use light troops to attack Baima to sneak attack its incomplete defenses, and Yan Liang will be captured.” Cao Cao did as Xun You said, with everything going as Xun You predicted. Yan Liang was killed in battle, relieving the siege. However, Cao Cao went west and ended up pursued by Wen Chou and his cavalry. At the time, Cao Cao sent for supplies from Baima. Many generals, knowing that Wen Chou would intercept them, urged Cao Cao to defend his camp and not move. Xun You, however, said, “This is what we are using to lure the enemy! How could we retreat?” Cao Cao looked at Xun You and then laughed. Cao Cao charged out as Wen Chou and his forces were distracted by the alluring supplies and inflicted a defeat, with Wen Chou killed in battle. At Guan ferry, Cao Cao faced off Yuan Shao, but he began running out of military supplies. Xun You suggested to Cao Cao, “The transport vehicles of Yuan Shao will arrive quite soon. He has a general, Han Xun. He is vigorous, but he underestimates his foes. Attack him and he can be defeated.” Cao Cao said, “Who can we send?” Xun You said, “We should have Xu Huang do so.” Xu Huang would successfully defeat Han Xun and the transporting vehicles were burned down. Soon after, Xu You came to surrender, telling Cao Cao to attack Chunyu Qiong, who was leading a massive army on behalf of Yuan Shao to retrieve the transport vehicles. The majority of the advisors were wary of Xu You, and only Xun You and Jia Xu urged Cao Cao to follow Xu You. Cao Cao chose to listen to them and attacked Chunyu Qiong, inflicting a crushing defeat on him. Xun You was left behind to defend the camp with Cao Hong. Soon, Zhang He and Gao Lan left Yuan Shao to surrender to Cao Hong, but Cao Hong refused to accept them out of doubt. Xun You told Cao Hong, “The plan Zhang He had was not adopted, so he came here in his resentment. Sir, why be doubtful?” Cao Hong then admitted the surrendering generals. In the entirety of the conflict between Cao Cao and Yuan Shao, Xun You and his quick thinking proved to be a major boon for Cao Cao.

Around 205, Xun You became District Marquis of Lingshu (陵樹亭侯) with a fief of three hundred households. Cao Cao submitted a petition urging so, stating, “Army Advisor Xun You has been an aiding official from the very beginning. There is no expedition he did not follow in. Every triumph over foes throughout time was planned by Xun You.”

In 207, Cao Cao discussed about officials who had notable achievements. He stated, “He who is loyal and upright, who has secret stratagems, and who can comfort and pacify both the inside and the outside is Xun Wenruo. Xun Gongda is his second.” Xun You was given an additional four hundred households, merging with the previous three hundred.

In the closing months of 213, Cao Cao was given permission to rule the semi-independent state of Wei as Duke by the Han dynasty. Xun You played a critical role in it by urging Cao Cao to assume the role. Xun You became its Prefect of the Secretariat. During his tenure, he recommended talented scholars to Cao Cao. However, he would not last long in this role. Xun You fell ill while travelling in a campaign against Sun Quan in the seventh lunar month of 214 and soon died. He was fifty-seven at the time. Cao Cao was devastated by his death and cried whenever he talked about him.

Xun You had at least two sons: Xun Ji and Xun Shi. Xun Ji was similar to Xun You in personality, but died before him. Xun Shi inherited the fiefdom of Lingshu, but he died without any descendants, thus terminating the fiefdom. During the Huangchu era (220–227), however, a grandson of Xun You, Xun Biao, was granted the title of District Marquis of Lingshu.

During the Zhengshi era, (239–249), Xun You was granted the posthumous title of the Respected Marquis. On 27 December 244, Xun You was given sacrifices at the temple of Cao Cao in recognition of his service. Perhaps it was this year Xun You also received his posthumous title.

In terms of personality, Xun You cared little about bettering his reputation and mostly kept to himself. He garnered praise from certain scholars for being perceived as less sanctimonious than Xun Yu. He thought out his plans carefully.

full_text:
Xun You (Gongda) 荀攸 (公達)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Xun You (荀攸), styledGongda (公達). He provided strategies instrumental in defeating Yuan Shao to Cao Cao. Xun You was affiliated with the Han Dynasty, Dong Zhuo, and the Wei Kingdom. Xun You lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.
